metadata.title: CRA Security Controls Catalog – prEN 40000-1-4 (PT2 Generic Security Requirements)
metadata.remarks: Derived from the CEN/CLC/JTC 13/WG 9 PT2 deep-dive session by Angelo D'Amato (Vulnir B.V.), 5 March 2026, under STAN4CR Grant Agreement No. 101196779. Maps CRA Annex I Part I Essential Requirements (a)–(m) to security objectives and technical controls from prEN 40000-1-4, building on EN 18031:2024 with new controls for CRA scope.

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Be made available on the market without known exploitable vulnerabilities.

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.KnownVulnerabilityExploitation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Ensure that the product is placed on the market without known exploitable vulnerabilities by maintaining a vulnerability management process including asset lists, EUVD monitoring, and supply-chain due diligence.

controls.title: [GEC-1] Up-to-date software and hardware without known exploitable vulnerabilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall be made available on the market with up-to-date software and hardware that do not contain publicly known exploitable vulnerabilities under practical operational conditions.

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Be made available on the market with a secure by default configuration, including the possibility to reset the product to its original state.

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.UnsecureDefaultConfigExploitation | Threat.StartupConfigExploitation | Threat.MissingResetFunctionalityConfigExploitation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Ensure the product is delivered with a secure default configuration, minimising exposed interfaces and services.

controls.title: [GEC-2] Limit exposure of services via related network interfaces

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall limit exposure of services via related network interfaces.

controls.title: [GEC-3] Configuration of optional services and related exposed network interfaces

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Optional services and their related exposed network interfaces shall be configurable.

controls.title: [GEC-4] Documentation of exposed network interfaces and services

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Exposed network interfaces and services shall be documented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall not include unnecessary external interfaces.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: External sensing capabilities shall be documented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall not include software components that are not required for its intended functionality.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall enforce password strength requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Factory default passwords shall meet defined strength requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Non-factory-default passwords shall meet defined strength requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall enforce a secure configuration during the startup process.

controls.title: [DLM-1] Applicability of deletion mechanisms (factory reset context)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Deletion mechanisms shall be applicable to support factory reset functionality.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall provide a factory reset function to restore the original state.

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Ensure that vulnerabilities can be addressed through security updates, including automatic updates enabled by default, with an opt-out mechanism, user notification, and the option to postpone updates.

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.UnpatchableVulnerabilityExploitation | Threat.UnpatchedVulnerabilityExploitation | Threat.MissingUpdateNotificationExploitation | Threat.FunctionalityUpdateInterruption

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall include applicable and appropriate update mechanisms.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Updates shall be delivered and installed securely.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall support automated security updates enabled by default, with a clear opt-out mechanism.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall notify users of available security updates.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall allow users to temporarily postpone security updates.

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Ensure protection from unauthorised access by appropriate control mechanisms including authentication, identity or access management systems, and report on possible unauthorised access.

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.UnauthorizedAccess | Threat.NotReportedUnauthorizedAccess

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall implement appropriate access control mechanisms.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall implement appropriate authentication mechanisms.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where one-factor authentication is used, the mechanism shall meet defined requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where multi-factor authentication is available, the mechanism shall meet defined requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall validate authenticators appropriately.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall support the changing of authenticators.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall implement brute force protection for authentication mechanisms.

controls.title: [GEC-13] Report on possible unauthorised access and corruption

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall report on possible unauthorised access attempts and detected corruption.

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Protect the confidentiality of stored, transmitted or otherwise processed data by state-of-the-art mechanisms such as encryption at rest and in transit.

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.DataAtRestDisclosure | Threat.DataProcessedDataDisclosure | Threat.DataInTransitDisclosure

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall implement applicable secure storage mechanisms to protect data confidentiality at rest.

controls.title: [SSM-3] Appropriate confidentiality protection for secure storage mechanisms

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Secure storage mechanisms shall provide appropriate confidentiality protection.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall protect the integrity of its software, firmware, and data during processing.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Secure communication mechanisms shall provide confidentiality protection for transmitted data.

controls.title: [SCM-4] Replay protection for secure communication mechanisms

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Secure communication mechanisms shall implement replay protection.

controls.title: [SCM-2] Appropriate integrity and authenticity protection for secure communication

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Secure communication mechanisms shall provide appropriate integrity and authenticity protection.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall use appropriate confidential cryptographic keys.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall implement appropriate cryptographic key generation mechanisms.

controls.title: [CCK-3] Preventing static default values for preinstalled CCKs

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Preinstalled confidential cryptographic keys shall not use static default values.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall use state-of-the-art (best practice) cryptographic mechanisms, including crypto-agility.

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Protect the integrity of stored, transmitted or otherwise processed data, commands, programs and configuration against unauthorised manipulation or modification, and report on corruptions.

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.DataAtRestTampering | Threat.DataInTransitTampering | Threat.ProcessedDataTampering | Threat.TamperingUndetected

controls.title: [SSM-2] Appropriate integrity protection for secure storage mechanisms

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Secure storage mechanisms shall provide appropriate integrity protection.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall protect the integrity of data and functions during processing.

controls.title: [SCM-2] Integrity and authenticity protection for communication (integrity context)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Secure communication mechanisms shall provide integrity protection for transmitted data.

controls.title: [GEC-13] Report on possible unauthorised access and corruption (integrity context)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall report on detected integrity violations and data corruption.

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Process only data, personal or other, that are adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary in relation to the intended purpose of the product with digital elements (data minimisation).

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.UnnecessaryDataMisuse

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall include applicable user notification mechanisms.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: User notifications shall contain appropriate content to inform the user about data processing activities.

controls.title: [DTM-1] Data minimisation in relation to intended purpose

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall only process data adequate, relevant and limited to what is necessary for its intended purpose.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall default to minimal data processing, with any additional processing requiring explicit user consent.

controls.title: [DTM-3] Configurable Controls for Optional Data Processing

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall provide configurable controls enabling users to manage optional data processing, including opt-in and opt-out mechanisms.

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Protect the availability of essential and basic functions, also after an incident, including through resilience and mitigation measures against denial-of-service attacks.

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.AvailabilityDegradationAfterIncident | Threat.AvailabilityDegradationDuringIncident

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall support recovery of essential and basic functions after a security incident.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall support control system backup to facilitate recovery after an incident.

controls.title: [RLM-1] Applicability and appropriateness of resilience mechanisms

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall include applicable and appropriate resilience mechanisms to maintain availability during incidents, including DoS attacks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall prioritize essential network traffic to maintain core functions during an incident.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall prioritize critical resources to maintain essential functions during an incident.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall implement resource management to prevent resource exhaustion attacks.

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Minimise the negative impact by the products themselves or connected devices on the availability of services provided by other devices or networks.

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.ExtServiceAvailabilityDegradation

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Product integrity shall be maintained to prevent the product from being used as a vector for external attacks.

controls.title: [TCM-1] Applicability of and appropriate traffic control mechanisms

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall implement applicable and appropriate traffic control mechanisms to manage and limit outgoing network traffic.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall implement mechanisms to limit its negative impact on external services and networks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall implement controls to prevent propagation of attacks to other devices or networks.

controls.title: [NMM-1] Applicability and appropriateness of network monitoring mechanisms

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall include applicable and appropriate network monitoring mechanisms to detect abnormal traffic patterns and potential external impact.

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Be designed, developed and produced to limit attack surfaces, including external interfaces.

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.UnnecessaryFunctionalityExploitation

controls.title: [GEC-2] Limit exposure of services via network interfaces (attack surface context)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Network service exposure shall be minimised to reduce the attack surface.

controls.title: [GEC-3] Configuration of optional services (attack surface context)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Optional services and interfaces shall be configurable and disabled when not needed.

controls.title: [GEC-4] Documentation of exposed network interfaces (attack surface context)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All exposed network interfaces and services shall be documented as part of attack surface analysis.

controls.title: [GEC-5] No unnecessary external interfaces (attack surface context)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unnecessary external interfaces shall be removed or disabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall validate all inputs to prevent exploitation via malformed data.

controls.title: [GEC-7] Documentation of external sensing capabilities (attack surface context)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: External sensing capabilities shall be documented as part of attack surface characterisation.

controls.title: [GEC-12] No Unneeded Software Components (attack surface context)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unneeded software components shall be removed to minimise the attack surface.

groups.title: ECR (k) – Reduce Impact of Incident (Hardening / Exploit Mitigations)

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Be designed, developed and produced to reduce the impact of an incident using appropriate exploitation mitigation mechanisms and techniques.

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.ExploitationMitigationFailure

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: Applicable exploit mitigation techniques include: Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR), Data Execution Prevention (DEP), Control-Flow Integrity (CFI), sandboxing, signed software execution policies, secure-by-default configuration, memory safety management, and least privilege. Operational controls include patch management, application whitelisting, network segmentation, and access control.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall implement appropriate exploit mitigation mechanisms and techniques to reduce the impact of successful attacks, including privilege escalation, lateral movement, malware installation, and injection exploits.

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Provide security related information by recording and monitoring relevant internal activity, including the access to or modification of data, services or functions, with an opt-out mechanism for the user.

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.NotRecordedSecurityActivity | Threat.NotMonitoredSecurityActivity | Threat.UnnecessaryDataLogging | Threat.UnnecessaryDataMonitoring

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall include applicable logging mechanisms for security-relevant events.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Log data shall be persistently stored to support post-incident analysis.

controls.title: [LGM-3] Minimum number of persistently stored events

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A defined minimum number of security-relevant events shall be persistently stored.

controls.title: [LGM-4] Time-related information of persistently stored log data

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Persistently stored log data shall include time-related information (timestamps) to support forensic analysis.

controls.title: [LGM-6] Exclude sensitive personal information from logs

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Log records shall exclude sensitive personal information unless strictly necessary, in line with data minimisation principles.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Network monitoring mechanisms shall detect security-relevant activities and anomalous behaviour.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall monitor security-relevant internal activities, including access to and modification of data, services and functions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall provide an opt-out mechanism allowing users to disable data logging where appropriate.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall provide an opt-out mechanism allowing users to disable data monitoring where appropriate.

props.name: requirement-text
props.value: Provide the possibility for users to securely and easily remove on a permanent basis all data and settings and, where such data can be transferred to other products or systems, ensure that this is done in a secure manner.

parts.name: threats
parts.prose: Threat.DeletedDataDisclosure | Threat.DataInTransitDisclosure | Threat.DataInTransitTampering

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The product shall provide applicable secure data deletion mechanisms enabling users to permanently and easily remove all data and settings.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When user data is transferred to other products or systems, confidentiality shall be protected during the transfer.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When user data is transferred to other products or systems, integrity shall be protected during the transfer.

controls.title: [DLM-4] Authenticity of the communication partner for export of data

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When user data is transferred to other products or systems, the authenticity of the receiving party shall be verified.
